headline: NGOs appeal to French state to stop economic support for Israel's occupation of Palestine
description: Five NGOs filed a legal appeal to require the French government to prevent any economic activity that might support Israel's settlement of Palestinian territories. The unprecedented legal move is based on the International Court of Justice's 2024 advisory opinion.

headline: Trump threatens Iran with escalation, including strikes on civilian and nuclear infrastructure
How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
European Commission fines Google €890 million, risking Trump's ire
description: The fine penalizes Google's practice of giving its own services more prominent treatment than those of its competitors.

headline: EU struggles to adopt new round of economic sanctions against Russia
description: The 27 member states reached an agreement on Thursday on a 21st package of sanctions against Moscow. Several member states asserted their individual interests and secured concessions.

headline: In Marseille, Chez Freddy's simple, sugar-coated chichis
description: 'Gourmet Kiosks' (2/6). Across France, small kiosks serving local specialties are a favorite stop for holidaymakers. Chez Freddy is a renowned stop for sweet fried dough.

headline: Salah Abdeslam, punished for protesting his prison conditions, challenges the administration
description: The last surviving member of the group behind the November 13, 2015, Paris terrorist attacks, Salah Abdeslam was sentenced to life without parole and solitary confinement for 10 years. He has now chosen to confront the authorities.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
description: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

headline: Zelensky forced to dismiss Oleksandr Syrsky, Ukraine's armed forces chief
description: Yielding to mounting pressure from demonstrators calling for General Syrsky's departure, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky reversed course and has replaced him with General Mykhailo Drapaty.

headline: European Commission withdraws €2 million grant from Venice Biennale over Russian presence
description: The major contemporary art event had allowed Russia to return to the festival and present a pavilion there in May, before closing it to the public, sparking controversy and leading the festival's jury to resign.

headline: France's agriculture law puts government in a bind
